app.name: Colorant Swipe
app.description: Swipe through FF14 colorants and discover your favorites

intro.lead: Pick the color you like {count} times.
intro.leadSuffix: We will recommend {count} colors for you.

about.title: About Colorant Swipe
about.p1: Colorant Swipe is a service for discovering FFXIV (FF14) dyes (colorants) you might like by picking between two colors at a time. From your choices it infers your preference using math in the OKLab color space and suggests 6 colors for you.
about.p2: No color theory knowledge required — just keep tapping the one you like. It's not a diagnosis, it's a chance to meet colors you wouldn't have picked yourself. Each result links to Dye Harmony so you can try real palettes built from that dye.

usage.title: How to Use
usage.step2: Pick the color you prefer from the two on screen (5 rounds). Swipe, tap, or use the ← → keys.
usage.step3: When you're done, 6 recommended colors appear as tiles.
usage.step4: Tap any color to open it in Dye Harmony and explore palettes built around it.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes, it's completely free with no account required.
faq.a2: No. It uses only OKLab color-space math: farthest-point sampling, preference vectors, Gaussian score propagation, and diversity constraints. Explainable and stable.
faq.a3: Pairwise comparisons carry a lot of information per answer, and combined with adaptive question selection, 5 is enough for usable accuracy. The short session is meant to be played many times.
faq.q4: Why do results differ even when my preferences stay the same?
faq.a4: Question pairs are sampled randomly from a representative pool, so each session probes your taste from slightly different angles. Results vary a bit too — that's part of the discovery.
faq.q5: Is this an official Square Enix tool?
faq.a5: No, this is an unofficial tool individually developed by a player, not affiliated with Square Enix.
